title: C++20 CPU Machine Learning Library
summary: From-scratch CPU machine learning library in C++20 with MLP, CNN, and RNN support, a float32 tensor core, dual-path GEMM, and end-to-end MNIST training with benchmarks and CI.
bullets: Built MLP, CNN, and RNN modules with manual forward/backward passes, a float32 tensor/allocator core, and reusable module APIs wired through all training binaries.

Implemented dual-path f32 GEMM using Apple Accelerate BLAS for large matmuls and custom ARM NEON blocked micro-kernels with autotuned tiling, multi-threaded execution, and dedicated weight-gradient kernels for linear, conv, and RNN layers.

Shipped convolution as an im2col-to-GEMM pipeline with NCHW layout transforms, parallel im2col/col2im, cached patch buffers for backward weight updates, and CPU-oriented fusion across the conv forward/backward hot path.

Trained MNIST to ~96% test accuracy in ~19s over 10 epochs on Apple Silicon (batch 128) using batched SGD and cross-entropy loss, with per-epoch timing, CSV metrics, and self-contained HTML training dashboards.

Added 13 CTest suites, GEMM reference equivalence tests, finite-difference gradient checks, GEMM/linear benchmarks, and macOS CI covering build, tests, example smoke runs, and benchmark smoke execution.

title: DC-UTPX
summary: Userspace UDP transport protocol for data-center incast workloads in C++, combining DCTCP-style ECN feedback, delay/loss congestion control, and microburst pacing.
bullets: 99.97% delivery ratio and P99 RTT < 2.2 ms across 73 concurrent flows under 16-sender fan-in stress.
9× aggregate throughput vs. kernel TCP under synchronized incast (856 Mbps vs. 95 Mbps).
eBPF-based per-flow telemetry for RTT percentiles, congestion window trajectories, and ECN-mark fractions.
Jain fairness index ≥ 0.945 and goodput loss < 0.003% across all workload scenarios.

title: GEMM Kernel
summary: C++20 GEMM kernel on Apple M2, progressing from naive to cache-blocked, NEON-SIMD, packed, and multithreaded implementations.
bullets: 231 GFLOP/s (95% of OpenBLAS) — a 223× speedup over baseline.
Optimized 8×8 ARM NEON FMA microkernels, increasing performance from 16 to 42.7 GFLOP/s.
L2-tuned, SIMD-aligned memory packing with no dynamic allocation in hot paths (2.84× throughput gain).
Parallelized across 8 cores using std::thread, scaling from 43 to 231 GFLOP/s.
